message:
fix(frankenterm,runtime_compat): surface select! macro publicly; plumb cx/cancellation + new config fields

Two follow-ups that keep the top-level binary compiling against the
current core surface.

runtime_compat: flip the re-export of `select!` from `pub(crate)` to
`pub`. The macro is used by consumers of the `frankenterm_core::
runtime_compat` module that live outside the crate (notably
`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` and the robot-SDK integration layer),
and under `pub(crate)` they could not name `runtime_compat::select!{...}`
from their own call sites. No body change, just visibility.

frankenterm/src/main.rs (the CLI binary):

- `distributed_agent_stream_session` / its `select!` branch handling
  `frankenterm_core::events::RecvError` now also matches the new
  `RecvError::Cancelled` variant introduced when core event-bus
  recv became cx-aware. On Cancelled the stream logs an `info!` with
  the agent_id, session_id, heartbeat count, and lag-repair count
  and returns `Ok(())`, so the session unwinds cleanly instead of
  bubbling a panic.

- Three `StorageConfig` literals in `run_distributed_agent`,
  `run_watcher`, and the inline workflow-runner block add the new
  `defer_fts_triggers: false` field so the struct literal matches
  the updated `frankenterm_core::storage::StorageConfig` surface.

- The `DiagnosticOptions` literal in `run` (the diagnostic-bundle
  codepath) adds `include_full_cwd: false` to match the
  `DiagnosticOptions` field added in the corresponding proptest
  update. Default remains "do not include full cwd".

- `execute_tx_rollback_with_executor` forwards the `contract`
  reference into `executor.execute_compensations(...)` since the
  trait method now takes the contract as its first argument so the
  compensator can inspect transaction state. The matching
  `execute_compensations` test impl (in the same file's `mod tests`)
  adds the new `_contract: &MissionTxContract` parameter so the
  mock implementation still satisfies the trait.

No public API additions in the binary, no behaviour changes for paths
that were already matching Cancelled or already passing the contract.
